heading: Operation of unauthorized unmanned aircraft over wildfires
label: 40A Operation of unauthorized unmanned aircraft over wildfires

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), an individual who operates an unmanned aircraft and knowingly or recklessly interferes with a wildfire suppression, or law enforcement or emergency response efforts related to a wildfire suppression, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 2 years, or both.1So in original. Probably should be “effort”.1

children.heading: .—Exceptions
children.label: b .—Exceptions
children.text: This section does not apply to the operation of an unmanned aircraft conducted by a unit or agency of the United States Government or of a State, tribal, or local government (including any individual conducting such operation pursuant to a contract or other agreement entered into with the unit or agency) for the purpose of protecting the public safety and welfare, including firefighting, law enforcement, or emergency response.

children.heading: .—Unmanned aircraft
children.label: 1 .—Unmanned aircraft
children.text: The term “unmanned aircraft” has the meaning given the term in , United States Code.section 44801 of title 49

children.heading: .—Wildfire
children.label: 2 .—Wildfire
children.text: The term “wildfire” has the meaning given that term in section 2 of the Emergency Wildfire Suppression Act ().42 U.S.C. 1856m

children.heading: .—Wildfire suppression
children.label: 3 .—Wildfire suppression
children.text: The term “wildfire suppression” means an effort to contain, extinguish, or suppress a wildfire.
